title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
During an emergency meeting, Detective Vane and the yacht owner, Stephen Megalos, engage in a heated argument regarding the progress of the investigation. Megalos criticizes Vane for failing to arrest the suspect, Crozack, while Vane accuses Megalos and others of being suspicious and withholding information. Vane specifically challenges Megalos regarding a story about three men from several years ago, suggesting Megalos has omitted crucial facts. The tension is heightened by Wain, who accuses the group of wasting time, and Dr. Temple, who faces criticism for his failed attempt to capture the Reinke couple.

To resolve the stalemate, Megalos proposes setting a trap for the anticipated attacker, Crozack. He suggests that the police should appear to withdraw their presence from the area to lure Crozack out of hiding. Although Vane is skeptical that Crozack will not suspect a ruse, he agrees to consider the plan and promises a decision by the morning. Megalas expresses his determination to face the danger rather than hide on his yacht, the *Helen*. Ellery observes that Megalas is acting like a fool.

The following morning, the situation turns catastrophic. Captain Swift is found bound and unconscious in his cabin with a head injury. Megalas is found dead; his headless body has been tied to a mast on the deck of the *Helen*. The group travels to the yacht in a state of shock and nausea to witness the horrific scene of the murder.
